strengths: Strong Tableau and Power BI visualization experience
Proven automation and ETL pipeline skills (Python, BigQuery, Snowflake)
Demonstrated data quality and governance implementation
Quantified impact with measurable business outcomes
weaknesses: No explicit Qualtrics or survey management experience
Relatively short domain-specific years in data roles
improvement_bullets: Designed and managed complex survey instruments and data collection protocols, utilizing platforms similar to Qualtrics to gather actionable feedback and drive program evaluation initiatives.
Drafted comprehensive data documentation, reporting guides, and visual process flows in Visio to standardize cross-functional data governance and support stakeholder accessibility.
Led survey design and data collection efforts for marketing campaigns, performing quality control to ensure data integrity and alignment with organizational evaluation frameworks.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to integrate program-specific tracking data (such as swim-tracking applications) into centralized reporting dashboards to monitor community impact.

Synthesized raw program data into meaningful insights through rigorous survey management, effectively translating evaluation findings into recommendations for operational and community-based improvements.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analyst (Association Office) opportunity with the YMCA of Greater New York. I am interested in this opportunity because of the YMCA's long-standing commitment to empowering youth and strengthening community through data-driven program evaluation. What interests me about working for the YMCA is its 'LEAP' career framework and its focus on a people-first approach, which excites me because I believe technology and data should ultimately serve the goal of improving lives. I believe my expertise in data automation and my commitment to rigorous program evaluation would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on creating a 'single source of truth' for business stakeholders. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I developed new reporting workflows that replaced manual processes, which helped me identify high-value customer segments through statistical modeling. This effort contributed to a 300% increase in revenue for targeted segments, demonstrating how I can translate raw data into the kind of measurable community impact the YMCA values. Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst (Association Office) role at the YMCA of Greater New York would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in SQL and Python to support the Research, Evaluation & Strategy department's mission. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my survey design and data documentation skills. I frequently designed A/B tests and organized complex reporting structures, which required me to document methodologies clearly to ensure team alignment. These projects resulted in improved reporting accuracy and faster strategic decision-making, results I am eager to replicate in your program initiatives. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in data governance in working on impact measurement and program evaluation at the YMCA. I know that my analytical rigor and dedication to data quality align with what it takes to succeed in the Data Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
